draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Real-time biometric feedback creates animated visual rhythm that mirrors the runner's body state
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid continuous motion vs snappy discrete updates
facade.label: Pace pulses with heart rate
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace animated state-syncing that breathes with the runner's physiology; transitions should feel organic and alive
facade.rejectImplies: Keep metrics static or update on fixed intervals; animations should not mimic biological rhythms

evidence.hypothesis: Real-time biometric feedback creates animated visual rhythm that mirrors the runner's body state
evidence.implication: Embrace animated state-syncing that breathes with the runner's physiology; transitions should feel organic and alive

facade.hypothesis: For a running app, a persistent left sidebar showing live metrics (pace, distance, HR) reduces cognitive load vs. tabbed navigation that hides context.
facade.label: Sidebar with running stats
facade.acceptImplies: User values always-visible data hierarchy and quick glance feedback during activity.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers full-screen focus on primary content with metrics surfaced only on demand.

facade.hypothesis: A runners' weather app could feel high-energy and synthetic—bright neon colors, sharp typefaces, dark background—prioritizing data readability and real-time urgency over warmth.
facade.label: Neon digital
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward tech-forward, performance-focused aesthetics; minimalist interface with crisp information hierarchy; possibly dark mode.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants softer, more natural visual language; warmer color palettes; possibly more photography or illustration; less 'instrument panel' feel.

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from high contrast, clean typography, and lots of white space—prioritizing readability over decoration, especially during outdoor use.
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs. dark, density vs. airiness
facade.label: Bright and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward clarity and simplicity; likely prefers glanceable data, high legibility in sunlight, and uncluttered interface.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer richer visual depth, darker palettes for evening runs, or more atmospheric/immersive design that doesn't feel clinical.

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer a voice that feels like a running buddy—encouraging and relatable—rather than corporate or robotic
facade.label: Casual, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Build personality into copy: 'You crushed it!' instead of 'Run logged.' Use contractions and active encouragement throughout the app.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward clean, neutral language. Let data and UI do the talking. Skip emoji and motivational framing—keep it straightforward and efficient.

facade.hypothesis: Runners need simultaneous access to current conditions AND real-time pacing targets, not sequential tabs
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data (side-by-side) vs sequential narrative (one metric at a time)
facade.label: Split screen: weather + pace guide
facade.acceptImplies: Users want information architecture that mirrors their decision-making flow: glance weather, adjust pace instantly. Design for parallel scanning, not linear reading.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer simplified, singular focus—either weather OR pacing per screen. Information should be layered/progressive, not competing for attention.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants energizing, action-oriented feel vs. calm, meditative atmosphere
Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, wind, UV)
Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants energizing, action-oriented feel vs. calm, meditative atmosphere
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, wind, UV)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Gesture recovery—when a runner pauses mid-route, the UI snaps back to active state with a springy, satisfying motion that signals readiness to resume without jarring
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid transition vs snappy instant state-change
facade.label: Elastic snap-back
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace physics-based bounce and anticipatory motion in pauses; make resumption feel inviting not abrupt
facade.rejectImplies: Prefer instant state switches and minimal micro-animation; let the runner focus on the road without visual distraction

draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
